north_star: qualified watch time per impression, constrained by satisfaction, originality, profitability, and policy

skills.name: youtube-business-orchestrator
skills.description: Orchestrates a faceless YouTube business from channel thesis through postmortem, routing work across specialist agents and enforcing CTR, AVD, originality, profitability, and policy gates.
triggers: Starting or restructuring a channel.
Selecting which concepts enter production.
Coordinating several specialist agents.
Resolving disagreements about audience, package, scope, risk, or revenue.
Running weekly greenlight, production, and postmortem reviews.
inputs: Locked channel thesis or an explicit request to create one.
Idea backlog and active-project state table.
Production capacity, cash constraints, and release obligations.
Latest channel analytics, experiment log, and revenue dashboard.
Open policy, rights, sponsor, or reputational risks.
outputs: Project state board with owner, hypothesis, risk, next artifact, and gate status.
Monthly operating review covering creative, audience, revenue, cost, and policy.

skills.name: channel-strategy-architect
skills.description: Defines the channel audience, repeatable promise, content pillars, format portfolio, faceless identity, competitive moat, and monetization thesis.
triggers: Launching a new channel or major channel pivot.
The audience is unclear or returning viewers are weak.
Video ideas perform individually but do not form a coherent library.
A new niche, language, market, or revenue model is being considered.
inputs: Founder capabilities, interests, credibility, constraints, and anti-goals.
Audience evidence and competitive analysis.
Production budget, available tools, and desired publishing capacity.
Revenue goals and acceptable time horizon.
Policy and rights boundaries.
outputs: Monetization ladder and first ten-video test.

skills.name: audience-market-researcher
skills.description: Builds evidence-backed audience profiles, viewing jobs, language maps, content gaps, and commercial-intent insights for YouTube strategy and ideation.
triggers: Creating or revising the channel thesis.
Developing a new content pillar, series, offer, or sponsor category.
Investigating weak audience fit, comments, or returning-viewer performance.
Searching for durable idea territories rather than one-off trends.
inputs: Research question and decision it will inform.
Candidate audience, niche, geography, and language.
Existing channel analytics, comments, surveys, emails, or community data.
Competitor and adjacent-channel list.
Privacy, legal, and data-access constraints.
outputs: Evidence table with source, confidence, and implication.

skills.name: trend-competitive-intelligence
skills.description: Finds current YouTube outliers, format shifts, cross-niche opportunities, competitive gaps, and trend half-lives without encouraging imitation or rights violations.
triggers: Building the idea backlog or quarterly strategy.
A format is declining or competitors are copying it.
The channel needs adjacent growth opportunities.
A breaking trend may create a time-sensitive video.
inputs: Channel thesis and content pillars.
Peer, aspirational, substitute, and cross-niche channel sets.
Time window and target region/language.
Available analytics tools and public data.
Production and policy constraints.
outputs: Trend opportunity briefs and saturation map.

skills.name: idea-format-developer
skills.description: Generates, scores, pressure-tests, and greenlights high-potential video concepts and repeatable formats for faceless entertainment-education channels.
triggers: Creating a backlog or selecting the next upload.
Developing a repeatable series or adapting an outlier format.
A channel has strong production but inconsistent views.
A script or package feels forced because the underlying idea is weak.
inputs: Locked channel thesis.
Audience evidence and current competitive/trend intelligence.
Previous postmortems and format performance.
Production capacity, budget, rights, and revenue constraints.
Any unique access, data, expertise, or assets.
outputs: Completed concept brief for each greenlight candidate.
Format definition with repeatability and variation rules.
Risk, proof, cost, and monetization notes.

skills.name: packaging-director
skills.description: Integrates titles and thumbnails into distinct packaging hypotheses that win qualified clicks and create explicit obligations for the opening and payoff.
triggers: A concept is greenlit.
CTR or impressions underperform while viewing quality is strong.
An evergreen video may benefit from repackaging.
A native YouTube A/B test is planned.
inputs: Concept brief, target viewer, target surface, and channel thesis.
Proof, access, and final payoff available in the video.
Competitive package audit and current channel baselines.
Thumbnail assets or capture plan.
Previous A/B and postmortem learnings.
outputs: Three title-thumbnail hypotheses with proof mapping.
Thumbnail capture/design brief and title bank assignments.
Opening obligations and A/B test plan.

skills.name: thumbnail-art-director
skills.description: Designs and briefs mobile-first thumbnails with one dominant idea, strong visual proof, clean hierarchy, originality, and testable alternatives for faceless channels.
triggers: A packaging brief requires thumbnail concepts or asset capture.
An existing thumbnail needs a new hypothesis.
A channel needs a recognizable but flexible visual system.
Thumbnail production is becoming slow, cluttered, or derivative.
inputs: Locked packaging brief and inferred viewer promise.
Available or creatable visual proof.
Channel visual system and competitor audit.
Rights-cleared source assets or capture plan.
Mobile display constraints and test plan.
outputs: Thumbnail creative brief and visual sentence.
Rough composition board and selected variants.
Asset capture/generation list with rights basis.
Production files, exports, and accessibility description.

skills.name: title-copywriter
skills.description: Writes concise, accurate, high-curiosity YouTube titles for browse and search, paired deliberately with thumbnail information and audience intent.
triggers: A packaging director needs title routes.
A search-led or browse-led video is being prepared.
An evergreen video needs repackaging.
Titles are verbose, generic, repetitive, or disconnected from the actual payoff.
inputs: Concept and packaging briefs.
Target viewer and traffic surface.
Thumbnail concepts and information they already communicate.
Audience language bank, search intent, and competitor audit.
Exact result, stakes, and proof available in the video.
outputs: Title bank grouped by hypothesis and surface.
Opening obligations created by each title.

skills.name: research-fact-checker
skills.description: Builds source-backed research packets, claim ledgers, counterevidence, uncertainty labels, visual proof plans, and final factual signoff for YouTube videos.
triggers: A concept contains factual, historical, scientific, financial, legal, medical, political, reputational, or current claims.
The script uses third-party footage, data, screenshots, quotes, or documents.
A sponsor claim or product comparison requires substantiation.
A video is being updated or corrected.
inputs: Concept and packaging briefs.
Research questions and intended conclusion.
Target audience sophistication and runtime.
Risk category, geography, and publication date.
Available source subscriptions, archives, interviews, and datasets.
outputs: Script and final-cut factual signoff or blocking corrections.

skills.name: story-retention-scriptwriter
skills.description: Writes package-first, evidence-backed YouTube scripts with strong first-30-second confirmation, causal progression, curiosity loops, proof, emotional variation, and proportionate payoff.
triggers: Packaging and research are ready for scripting.
Intro retention or mid-video AVD is weak.
An essay, article, or research packet must become video-native.
A faceless format needs recurring story architecture.
inputs: Locked packaging brief and opening obligations.
Research packet and visual proof opportunities.
Target viewer, surface, runtime, and brand voice.
Sponsor/CTA requirements and policy constraints.
Retention postmortems from comparable videos.
outputs: Retention map with beat timing and loop closures.
Optional-cut map and sponsor integration location.

skills.name: faceless-visual-producer
skills.description: Plans and produces original, rights-cleared visuals, diagrams, screen captures, motion systems, archival transformations, and proof assets for faceless YouTube videos.
triggers: A locked script needs a storyboard or asset plan.
A channel is overly dependent on generic stock clips.
Custom graphics, maps, data, screens, demonstrations, or archival material are required.
The visual system must become faster without becoming templated.
inputs: Script, retention map, and visual proof list.
Channel visual grammar and brand standards.
Asset budget, tools, team capability, and deadline.
Rights and disclosure requirements.
Thumbnail capture requests.
outputs: Original and licensed asset package with editable sources.
Thumbnail capture assets and disclosure notes.

skills.name: voice-sound-producer
skills.description: Produces clear, distinctive narration, music, sound design, pronunciation, pacing, and final audio mixes for faceless YouTube videos while respecting voice rights and disclosure rules.
triggers: A script is locked for narration.
The channel uses human or synthetic voiceover.
Retention suffers from monotone delivery, poor intelligibility, or exhausting sound design.
Music or voice licensing and disclosure must be managed.
inputs: Locked script with emotional and pronunciation notes.
Brand voice profile and target viewer.
Reference mix, music/SFX licenses, and platform delivery specifications.
AI disclosure and voice-consent requirements.
Picture timing or animatic where available.
outputs: Dialogue, music, effects stems and final mix.

skills.name: retention-video-editor
skills.description: Edits faceless YouTube videos for promise match, clarity, causal story, proof, emotional pacing, and qualified AVD rather than indiscriminate speed or visual noise.
triggers: Assets and narration are ready for rough cut.
A rough cut feels slow, confusing, repetitive, or overstimulating.
Past videos show intro drops, mid-video dips, or sponsor-related abandonment.
A final retention and publish-readiness review is required.
inputs: Locked package, script, retention map, storyboard, assets, narration, and sponsor requirements.
Research packet and rights manifest.
Target runtime and previous retention postmortems.
Brand editing grammar and delivery specifications.
outputs: Story rough cut and review notes.
Final master, clean master, captions, stems, and project archive.
Predicted retention risks and optional recut plan.

skills.name: publishing-seo-distribution
skills.description: Builds complete upload packets, metadata, chapters, captions, playlists, end screens, native A/B tests, and coordinated distribution for long-form YouTube videos.
triggers: A final cut and package are approved.
A search-led evergreen video needs intent alignment.
A video requires chapters, playlists, end screens, disclosures, or external launch assets.
An older library needs metadata or packaging maintenance.
inputs: Final master and approved title/thumbnail variants.
Upload packet template, captions, chapters, source/credit requirements, and disclosures.
Target surface, search intent, playlist/series map, and next-video destination.
Monetization and sponsor settings.
Launch timing and distribution channels.
outputs: Published video record and variant configuration.
Description, chapters, captions, playlist, cards, end screen, pinned comment, and distribution copy.
Launch QA checklist and issue log.

skills.name: shorts-repurposing-strategist
skills.description: Creates original YouTube Shorts and long-form derivative assets with swipe-stopping first frames, compressed story, loops, standalone value, and deliberate bridges to the channel.
triggers: A long-form project contains short, standalone moments.
The channel needs top-of-funnel experiments or faster idea testing.
A Shorts-native format or series is being developed.
Viewed-versus-swiped, completion, or repeat performance is weak.
inputs: Long-form script/cut or Shorts-native concept.
Target viewer, first-frame visual, and central payoff.
Rights and music constraints.
Channel bridge objective and related long-form destination.
Past Shorts analytics.
outputs: Shorts brief with first-frame and payoff.
Vertical script/cut list and visual reframe plan.
Shorts postmortem and long-form transfer hypothesis.

skills.name: analytics-experimentation-scientist
skills.description: Designs YouTube experiments, interprets CTR and retention by context, diagnoses bottlenecks, separates causality from correlation, and converts every upload into a decision.
triggers: Before publication, to define predictions and tests.
After publication, to review packaging, retention, audience fit, and revenue.
When performance is ambiguous or team narratives conflict.
When deciding whether to scale, revise, repackage, sequel, or stop a format.
inputs: Pre-publish hypothesis, predicted band, package variants, and target surface.
YouTube Analytics data by video, source, audience, geography, and time.
Retention curves, A/B results, comments, distribution changes, and revenue/cost data.
Comparable channel baselines and similar-length videos.
Experiment/change log.
outputs: Primary diagnosis with confidence and alternative explanations.

skills.name: monetization-revenue-architect
skills.description: Designs an audience-aligned revenue stack across ads, Premium, sponsors, affiliates, products, memberships, licensing, and commerce while protecting retention and trust.
triggers: Creating the channel business model.
Approaching or entering YPP.
Adding sponsors, affiliates, products, memberships, or licensing.
RPM is weak, revenue is concentrated, or monetization harms retention.
inputs: Channel thesis, audience intent, geography, and advertiser suitability.
Current eligibility, revenue analytics, and traffic mix.
Product/sponsor categories, affiliate programs, and owned capabilities.
Production cost and cash requirements.
Trust, legal, and disclosure constraints.
outputs: Revenue architecture by pillar and viewer intent.

skills.name: sponsorship-affiliate-sales
skills.description: Sources, qualifies, prices, negotiates, integrates, discloses, and measures sponsorships and affiliate offers that fit the audience and preserve editorial trust.
triggers: The channel has a defined audience and repeatable viewership.
A sponsor, agency, affiliate program, or brand approaches.
A campaign needs pricing, negotiation, integration, tracking, or reporting.
Commercial segments show retention damage or weak conversion.
inputs: Audience/channel media kit and recent performance distribution.
Target brand categories and audience problem map.
Video slate and integration opportunities.
Rate floor, inventory, exclusivity limits, usage-rights policy, and legal terms.
Disclosure and advertiser-suitability requirements.
outputs: Integration script, disclosure, and tracking plan.

skills.name: community-audience-development
skills.description: Develops returning viewers and audience intelligence through comments, posts, series rituals, feedback loops, community programming, and next-video relationships.
triggers: Returning viewers or next-video movement are weak.
The channel needs audience feedback, community posts, or recurring rituals.
A series, membership, live element, or newsletter is being considered.
Comments reveal confusion, demand, or trust issues.
inputs: Channel thesis and brand voice.
Recent uploads, comments, audience analytics, community tools, and moderation policies.
Upcoming slate and next-video bridges.
Monetization/community plans.
Safety and privacy constraints.
outputs: Audience questions, language, and correction log.

skills.name: rights-policy-brand-safety
skills.description: Verifies current YouTube policy, originality, copyright, licensing, AI disclosure, paid promotion, advertiser suitability, privacy, and reputational risk with authority to stop publication.
triggers: Any video uses third-party assets, AI-generated realistic media, sponsor claims, sensitive topics, or personal data.
The channel is applying for or maintaining monetization.
A copyright claim, policy warning, demonetization, takedown, or dispute occurs.
A final publish-readiness review is required.
inputs: Concept, package, research packet, script, final cut, asset manifest, and upload packet.
Licenses, receipts, permissions, consent, voice/likeness records, and source archives.
Target territories, audience age, sponsors, and monetization settings.
Current official YouTube and applicable legal guidance.
outputs: Rights ledger audit and missing-proof list.
Advertiser, sponsor, privacy, and reputation review.
Final PASS/BLOCK/ESCALATE decision and incident log.

skills.name: production-operations-manager
skills.description: Plans capacity, schedules stage-gated production, manages vendors and files, controls scope and revisions, and improves throughput without lowering creative or compliance standards.
triggers: Multiple videos or agents are active.
Deadlines slip, revisions multiply, or assets become disorganized.
The team is hiring freelancers or outsourcing production.
Production cost or cycle time needs improvement.
inputs: Orchestrator priority list and project states.
Role capacity, availability, rates, and competencies.
Production estimates, dependencies, and vendor agreements.
File, review, security, and rights requirements.
Historical cycle-time and revision data.
outputs: Project briefs, vendor briefs, and review SLAs.
Cycle-time, revision, cost, and bottleneck report.

skills.name: finance-unit-economics
skills.description: Builds budgets, forecasts, video and format P&Ls, cash-flow scenarios, hiring thresholds, contribution metrics, and investment decisions for a YouTube media business.
triggers: Creating a channel business plan or production budget.
Greenlighting expensive concepts or hiring.
Evaluating sponsors, products, affiliates, or tools.
Revenue is growing but cash or profit is unclear.
inputs: Channel strategy, format slate, production estimates, and capacity.
Actual views, impressions, watch time, revenue by stream, and payment timing.
Vendor, labor, software, licensing, travel, product, and overhead costs.
Tax, currency, refund, commission, and working-capital assumptions.
Risk ranges and scenario inputs.
outputs: Hiring, tool, and outsourcing business cases.
Greenlight cost range and postmortem variance report.

skills.name: ai-pattern-mining-analyst
skills.description: Uses AI-assisted analysis to extract YouTube title, thumbnail, hook, comment, and format patterns from examples while preventing cloning, generic averaging, and unsupported factual claims.
triggers: Competitive examples need to become a pattern library.
Titles, thumbnails, hooks, comments, or first-30-second examples need structured analysis.
An agent needs original variants after outlier research.
Clone-risk review is required before using generated packages, hooks, or concepts.
inputs: Example titles, thumbnail descriptions, hooks, scripts excerpts, comments, or channel observations.
Analysis goal: title, thumbnail, hook, full format, comment gap, or script tone.
Channel thesis, target viewer, and brand voice card when available.
Rights, policy, originality, and factual-risk constraints.
outputs: Pattern report with viewer behavior, emotional packaging, and evidence notes.
Research, policy, packaging, or ideation handoff notes.

skills.name: sleep-channel-architect
skills.description: Designs scalable sleep, bedtime-story, calm-history, ambient documentary, and background-listening YouTube channels with original scripts, varied subjects, low-arousal packaging, and monetization-safe production rules.
triggers: Launching or repositioning a sleep, calm-history, bedtime-story, ambient documentary, or meditation-adjacent channel.
A channel needs long-session viewing without repetitive-template risk.
Sleep videos are too generic, too stimulating, or too similar across uploads.
A validation slate is needed for a sleep channel.
inputs: Channel goal, sleep subniche, target audience, and risk tolerance.
Production budget, voice type, visual budget, and upload cadence.
Competitive or outlier research where available.
Policy, rights, AI disclosure, and monetization constraints.
outputs: Brand, voice, ambience, visual, and metadata standards.
Monetization path and risk review cadence.

skills.name: sleep-scriptwriter
skills.description: Writes long-form low-arousal sleep, bedtime-story, calm-history, ambient documentary, and background-listening scripts with soft hooks, chapter loops, source-aware narration, visual/audio notes, and anti-repetition safeguards.
triggers: Writing or rewriting a sleep, calm-history, bedtime-story, ambient documentary, or background-listening script.
A normal documentary script needs a low-arousal sleep version.
Hooks, CTAs, visuals, or audio notes need to be softened for sleep continuity.
A sleep script needs chapter structure, source notes, and policy-safe originality review before production.
inputs: Sleep channel blueprint or explicit sleep-state promise.
Locked title/package, topic, audience, target length, and chapter length range.
Voice card, research packet, source boundaries, and do-not-claim list.
Ambience, visual style, CTA constraints, and policy notes.
outputs: Chapter map with gentle questions, sensory anchors, soft payoffs, and transition lines.
Full narration with voice, visual, ambience, source, and policy annotations.
Sleep rewrite notes and unresolved research questions.
